description: This schematic illustrates three types of oxide nodule structures formed on Fe-Mn-Al alloys after oxidation at 800 °C. Type 1 corresponds to large iron oxide nodules formed on alloys with <5 wt% Mn, consisting of an outer Fe₂O₃ layer, inner (Fe,Mn)₃O₄ phase, and internal oxide region containing (Fe,Mn)Al₂O₄ or Al₂O₃, with Al₂O₃ present on the base alloy surface. Type 2 refers to large iron oxide nodules formed on alloys with 5~7.5 wt% Mn, featuring discontinuous Mn₂O₃ layers/particles on the outermost surface, Fe₂O₃ as the sub-layer, (Fe,Mn)₃O₄ inside, and a partial healing layer of (Fe,Mn)Al₂O₄ or Al₂O₃ at the nodule-alloy interface. Type 3 are small Mn-rich nodules formed on alloys with ≥7.5 wt% Mn, with a continuous outer Mn₂O₃ layer, inner Fe₂O₃ phase, and a complete healing layer of (Fe,Mn)Al₂O₄ or Al₂O₃ at the base. This figure demonstrates that alloy Mn content determines nodule structure: higher Mn promotes continuous Mn₂O₃ outer layers and complete alumina/aluminate healing layers, retarding further nodule growth and improving oxidation resistance.
